---
title: "_03_control_studies"
author: "Josefina Weinerova"
date: "2025-10-05"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
# package loading
```{r}
# install.packages("openalexR")
if (!require(pacman)) install.packages("pacman")

pacman::p_load(
  openalexR, cowplot, tidyverse, httr, jsonlite,
  citecorp, rcrossref, purrr, dplyr, tibble, fect, furrr, future, data.table,
  panelView
)
```

#data loading
```{r}
matched_journals <- readRDS("data/matched_journals_pilot.rds")
df_meta <- readRDS("data/metadata_OpenAlex.rds")

```

# getting control studies

```{r}
# function to clean up the issn
clean_issn <- function(x, validate = TRUE, repair_missing_check = FALSE) {
  y <- str_to_upper(x)
  y <- str_replace_all(y, "[^0-9X]", "") # keep only digits/X

  # Must be 8 chars now to be considered an ISSN
  y[nchar(y) != 8] <- NA_character_

  # Insert hyphen
  y <- ifelse(is.na(y), NA_character_, paste0(substr(y, 1, 4), "-", substr(y, 5, 8)))

  y
}
```

```{r}
# Tidy split + normalize + dedupe
get_matches <- matched_journals %>%
  select(publication_year, issn) %>%
  tidyr::separate_rows(issn, sep = "\\s*,\\s*") %>% # split to rows
  mutate(
    year = suppressWarnings(as.integer(publication_year)),
    issn = clean_issn(issn, validate = TRUE, repair_missing_check = FALSE)
  ) %>%
  distinct(year, issn, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  filter(!is.na(issn), !is.na(year))


range(get_matches$year)
```

# query oa_fetch in batches
```{r}
email <- Sys.getenv("MY_EMAIL")
options(openalexR.mailto = email)

# assumes get_matches has columns: issn (e.g., "1234-5678") and year (integer)
batch_size <- 50L
rows_idx <- split(
  seq_len(nrow(get_matches)),
  ceiling(seq_along(seq_len(nrow(get_matches))) / batch_size)
)

results <- list()

for (b in seq_along(rows_idx)) {
  idxs <- rows_idx[[b]]
  message(sprintf(
    "Batch %d/%d: rows %d–%d",
    b, length(rows_idx), min(idxs), max(idxs)
  ))

  for (j in seq_along(idxs)) {
    r <- idxs[j]
    issn <- get_matches$issn[r]
    yr <- get_matches$year[r]

    message(sprintf(
      "  Row %d in batch %d: ISSN=%s, year=%s",
      j, b, issn, yr
    ))

    out <- try(
      oa_fetch(
        entity = "works",
        `primary_location.source.issn` = issn, # one ISSN (this row)
        publication_year = yr, # one year  (this row)
        type = "article",
        per_page = 200,
        options = list(
          select = c("id", "doi", "display_name", "publication_year", "primary_location")
        ),
        verbose = TRUE
      ),
      silent = TRUE
    )

    if (!inherits(out, "try-error") && is.data.frame(out) && nrow(out) > 0) {
      results[[length(results) + 1]] <- out
    } else {
      warning(sprintf("    No data / failed for ISSN=%s, year=%s", issn, yr))
    }

    Sys.sleep(0.2) # small pause between row-queries
  }

  Sys.sleep(0.8) # small pause between 50-row batches
}

matched_articles <- bind_rows(results) %>%
  distinct(id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  rename(title = display_name)

length(unique(matched_articles$issn_l))
length(unique(get_matches$issn))
matched_articles
```

# get missing metadata after the first scrape

```{r}
# checking if still missing some
pairs1 <- unique(matched_articles[c("publication_year", "issn_l")])
pairs2 <- unique(matched_journals[c("publication_year", "issn")])
pairs2 <- pairs2 %>% separate(issn, into = c("issn1", "issn2"))
pairs2 <- pairs2 %>%
  mutate(issn1 = str_c(str_sub(issn1, 1, 4), "-", str_sub(issn1, 5, 8))) %>%
  mutate(issn2 = str_c(str_sub(issn2, 1, 4), "-", str_sub(issn2, 5, 8)))
# which combo of year and issn is not in the articles but is in the journals
first_pairs <- pairs1 %>%
  transmute(publication_year = publication_year, issn = issn_l)
df2_key <- pairs2 %>% mutate(.row = row_number())

has_match_rows <- df2_key %>%
  pivot_longer(c(issn1, issn2), names_to = "which", values_to = "issn") %>%
  filter(!is.na(issn), nzchar(issn)) %>%
  semi_join(first_pairs, by = c("publication_year", "issn")) %>%
  distinct(.row)

rows_not_in_first <- df2_key %>%
  anti_join(has_match_rows, by = ".row")

get <- rows_not_in_first %>%
  select(publication_year, issn1, issn2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(issn1, issn2), values_to = "issn")
get <- get %>% filter(issn != "-")

# get metadata for missing articles
out_miss <- oa_fetch(
  entity = "works",
  `primary_location.source.issn` = get$issn,
  publication_year = get$publication_year,
  type = "article",
  options = list(
    select = c("id", "doi", "display_name", "publication_year", "primary_location")
  ),
  verbose = TRUE
)
out_miss <- out_miss %>% rename(title = display_name)
matched_articles <- dplyr::select(matched_articles, -license)
all_matched_articles <- rbind(matched_articles, out_miss)
all_matched_articles <- all_matched_articles %>% mutate(doi = stringr::str_remove(doi, regex("^https?://(dx\\.)?doi\\.org/", TRUE)))

# MISSING SOME OF THE JOURNAL MATCHED DOIS
## need to move this earlier for smoother workflow
miss_doi <- setdiff(df_meta$doi_o, all_matched_articles$doi)
df_meta%>%filter(doi_o=="10.1007/s10802-020-00712-w")
get2 <- df_meta %>%
  filter(doi_o %in% miss_doi) %>%
  select(publication_year, issn_l)
get2 <-unique(get2)

out_miss2 <- oa_fetch(
  entity = "works",
  `primary_location.source.issn` = get2$issn_l, # Nature's ISSN-L (example)
  publication_year = get2$publication_year,
  type="article",
  options = list(
    select = c("id", "doi", "display_name", "publication_year", "primary_location")
  ),
  verbose = TRUE
)

out_miss2 <- out_miss2 %>% rename(title = display_name)
out_miss2 <- dplyr::select(out_miss2, -license)
all_matched_articles <- rbind(all_matched_articles, out_miss2)
all_matched_articles <- all_matched_articles %>% mutate(doi = stringr::str_remove(doi, regex("^https?://(dx\\.)?doi\\.org/", TRUE)))

setdiff(df_meta$doi_o, all_matched_articles$doi)
```
#save ouput
```{r}
saveRDS(all_matched_articles, "data/all_matched_articles_pilot.rds")

```
